description:
You are a penetration tester who has already executed an Nmap scan.  
  The raw scan results are stored in the file  

    /home/user/scan-results/nmap_output.gnmap  

  Your goal is to generate human-readable, **lint-clean** Markdown
  documentation from that raw output **with one compound shell
  command** (pipelines, «;» or «&&» are allowed, but everything must be
  typed and executed as one line).

  1. Parse every “open” port entry inside
     /home/user/scan-results/nmap_output.gnmap and create a new file  

        /home/user/scan-results/OpenPorts.md  

     having exactly the following format:

        • The very first line **must** be  
          `# Open Ports Report`

        • A blank line follows.

        • Then a Markdown table using pipes (|) and the header row
          shown below.  The header separator row must contain at least
          three dashes per column.  Example:

          | Host | Port | Service |
          |------|------|---------|

        • After the separator line, add one data row for **each
          individual open port** discovered, sorted in the order they
          appear in the scan file.  Each row contains:

             Column 1: IP address  
             Column 2: portNumber/transportProtocol  
             Column 3: service name

          Example data row (spacing must match):

          | 10.10.0.5 | 21/tcp | ftp |

     IMPORTANT:  
     • Do not include “Status: Up” lines, closed ports or anything
       except open ports.  
     • Do not reorder or de-duplicate; simply preserve the appearance
       order from the scan file.

  2. Immediately after writing OpenPorts.md, run a quick lint that
     flags any Markdown line longer than 80 characters.  
     Save the lint findings to

        /home/user/scan-results/lint_report.log  

     One finding per line, with this exact format (no extra spaces):

        <lineNumber>:<lineLength>:<entire line content>

     If no line exceeds 80 characters the log file must be created but
     left completely empty (size 0).

  Constraints
  • Everything (Markdown generation **and** linting) must be carried
    out with a single, compound terminal command entered once.
  • Do **not** modify the original nmap_output.gnmap.
  • Only /home/user/scan-results/OpenPorts.md and
    /home/user/scan-results/lint_report.log should be created or
    touched.
  • The automated grader will strictly verify:
      – the presence, path and exact textual contents of
        OpenPorts.md;  
      – the presence of lint_report.log and that its size is 0 bytes.

  Once finished, you can cat the generated Markdown to inspect it, but
  that is optional.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing file placed for the agent -->
  File: /home/user/scan-results/nmap_output.gnmap
  Exact contents (including blank lines):

# Nmap 7.80 scan initiated
Host: 10.10.0.5 ()  Status: Up
Host: 10.10.0.5 ()  Ports: 21/open/tcp//ftp///, 80/open/tcp//http///, 8080/open/tcp//http-proxy///
Host: 10.10.0.15 () Status: Up
Host: 10.10.0.15 () Ports: 22/open/tcp//ssh///, 139/open/tcp//netbios-ssn///, 445/open/tcp//microsoft-ds///
# Nmap done

  -------------------------------------------------------------------

  File that must be created by the agent:
    /home/user/scan-results/OpenPorts.md

  Required **exact** content:

# Open Ports Report

| Host | Port | Service |
|------|------|---------|
| 10.10.0.5 | 21/tcp | ftp |
| 10.10.0.5 | 80/tcp | http |
| 10.10.0.5 | 8080/tcp | http-proxy |
| 10.10.0.15 | 22/tcp | ssh |
| 10.10.0.15 | 139/tcp | netbios-ssn |
| 10.10.0.15 | 445/tcp | microsoft-ds |

  -------------------------------------------------------------------

  File that must also be created by the agent:
    /home/user/scan-results/lint_report.log

  It must exist and be **exactly zero bytes** in size (empty), because
  every line in OpenPorts.md is shorter than 80 characters.

  -------------------------------------------------------------------

  Permissions:
    All files under /home/user/scan-results/ are owned by user:user and
    writable by the user (mode 0644 expected for new files).
name: task_000000_38aa2e9e
